completed_tasks.name: Spawn gsd-plan-checker (iteration 0)
completed_tasks.result: ISSUES FOUND — 1 BLOCKER + 4 WARNINGS + 1 INFO; all about malformed acceptance-criteria shell expressions in 05-03 and 05-04. Plans are otherwise structurally sound: all 4 requirements covered, all 18 D-IDs honored, wave graph clean, threat models present, action pins / attestation predicate / no-:latest / no-QEMU / cancel-in-progress: false / fail-loud GIT_SHA all correctly enforced.

Requirements coverage gate (§13) + decision coverage gate (§13a)
Annotate ROADMAP with wave dependencies / cross-cutting constraints (§13c)

decisions.decision: Selected 'Research first' at the research gate
decisions.rationale: CONTEXT.md leaves Claude-Discretion items (action version pins, jq syntax, verify-job timeout) — research grounds them. Outcome: research surfaced two CONTEXT.md corrections that the planner applied.

decisions.decision: Two CONTEXT.md corrections must persist across the revision pass
decisions.rationale: Plans 05-01..05-04 already use the corrected values. Revision must NOT regress them: (a) attestation jq predicate uses annotation `vnd.docker.reference.type=attestation-manifest`, NOT mediaType `application/vnd.in-toto+json`; (b) action pins are `actions/checkout@v6`, `docker/setup-buildx-action@v4`, `docker/login-action@v4`, `docker/metadata-action@v6`, `docker/build-push-action@v7`, `actions/upload-artifact@v7`, `actions/download-artifact@v8` — NOT the v5/v6 stale hint in CONTEXT.md.

decisions.decision: UI safety gate skipped per project config
decisions.rationale: workflow.ui_phase=false and workflow.ui_safety_gate=false in .planning/config.json — Phase 5 deliverables are workflow YAML + compose stub, no UI surface.

decisions.decision: Schema-push gate skipped — no ORM files in phase scope
decisions.rationale: Phase 5 touches .github/workflows/*.yml + docker-compose.prod.yml; no Payload/Prisma/Drizzle/Supabase/TypeORM schema deltas.

decisions.decision: Security threat model gate active (ASVS L1, block on high)
decisions.rationale: workflow.security_enforcement is unset, defaulting to enabled. Each PLAN.md includes a <threat_model> block. All 4 plans pass — high-severity threats (T-05-18 attestation predicate correctness, T-05-19/T-05-22/T-05-23 branch-protection drift) all have explicit mitigations.

checker_findings_to_fix.issue: Acceptance criterion uses `grep -F "ubuntu-latest && 'ubuntu-latest' || 'ubuntu-24.04-arm'"` but the actual YAML emits `'linux/amd64' && 'ubuntu-latest' || 'ubuntu-24.04-arm'`. The grep substring will not match — false-negative blocks Task 2 even when implementation is correct.
checker_findings_to_fix.fix_hint: Replace with `grep -F "matrix.platform == 'linux/amd64' && 'ubuntu-latest' || 'ubuntu-24.04-arm'" .github/workflows/ci.yml` exits 0. OR drop entirely — line 408 (`platform: [linux/amd64, linux/arm64]`) and line 409 (`grep -F 'ubuntu-24.04-arm'`) already cover D-10's native-runner mandate.

checker_findings_to_fix.issue: Redundant OR'd grep with mismatched quoting: `grep -F 'tr "[:upper:]" "[:lower:]"' OR grep -F "tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'"`. Actual YAML emits `tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'`; the OR phrasing is non-executable in shell.
checker_findings_to_fix.fix_hint: Reduce to single matching grep: `grep -F "tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'" .github/workflows/ci.yml` exits 0.

checker_findings_to_fix.issue: Self-falsifying pipeline `grep -F 'continue-on-error: true' | grep -v pip-audit` asserts exit 1, but `continue-on-error: true` lives on its OWN line (no `pip-audit` substring), so the second grep can't filter it. Acceptance will report exit 0 even when invariant holds.
checker_findings_to_fix.fix_hint: Either anchor on step context with awk: `! awk '/^      - name:/{n=$0} /continue-on-error: true/{print n}' .github/workflows/ci.yml | grep -v 'pip-audit'`. OR simplify to count: `test "$(grep -c 'continue-on-error: true' .github/workflows/ci.yml)" = 1` (asserting exactly one occurrence — the existing pip-audit step).

checker_findings_to_fix.issue: `grep -c '^  test:$\|^  compose-validate:$\|...'` uses BRE alternation `\|` without `-E`, which is treated as literal in default BRE. Output will be 0 even when all 5 jobs are present.
checker_findings_to_fix.fix_hint: Switch to extended regex: `grep -cE '^  (test|compose-validate|build|merge|verify):$' .github/workflows/ci.yml` outputs 5. OR use 5 separate `grep -c '^  X:$'` checks each asserting 1.

checker_findings_to_fix.issue: Awk range `/^## Image Publishing/,/^## Contributing$/` is positional but inclusive of both delimiters. If runbook lands AFTER `## Contributing`, the assertion `wc -l >= 2` may still pass on partial match. Redundant with line 214's awk range.
checker_findings_to_fix.fix_hint: Use line-number ordering: `D=$(grep -n '^## Documentation$' README.md | cut -d: -f1); I=$(grep -n '^## Image Publishing' README.md | cut -d: -f1); C=$(grep -n '^## Contributing$' README.md | cut -d: -f1); test "$D" -lt "$I" && test "$I" -lt "$C"`.

checker_findings_to_fix.issue: Closing code-fence on the embedded compose-validate YAML block has 6-space indent instead of 4; markdown still parses it correctly, but if executor literally pastes the fenced markdown the indentation could leak. Likely non-issue.
checker_findings_to_fix.fix_hint: No plan edit required — Task 1 acceptance already greps for `^  compose-validate:$` (2-space indent) which catches any indent leak.

next_action: Re-enter /gsd-plan-phase workflow at step 12 (revision loop iteration 1/3). Spawn gsd-planner with revision_context that lists the 5 fixes from `checker_findings_to_fix` with their plan/line/fix_hint values verbatim. After planner returns, re-spawn gsd-plan-checker (step 10), then proceed to coverage gates (§13/§13a), STATE.md update (§13b), ROADMAP annotation (§13c), commit (§13d), post-planning gap analysis (§13e), and final status (§14).
context_notes: Plans are structurally excellent — coverage is complete, action pins and attestation predicate use the corrected values, threat models present, manual-operator actions correctly encoded as `autonomous: false` with checkpoint:human-action. The checker's 5 findings are all malformed acceptance-criteria shell expressions in 05-03 and 05-04 — they don't change what gets built, only how completion is verified. The BLOCKER (#1) is the only one that would cause a real false-negative; the 4 WARNINGS would inflate revision-loop traffic during /gsd-execute-phase. Iteration count starts at 1; max 3 per workflow.
